_status: SKELETON — collection-level fields are real and verified via web search; the box/folder inventory below is empty because archives.lib.duke.edu blocks automated fetching (Anubis bot-detection challenge, confirmed on two separate fetch attempts plus a disconnected browser-automation attempt on 2026-07-08). A human browsing the finding_aid_url normally is not blocked. Paste the real box/folder listing into `boxes` before treating this as demo data, or leave it empty and build ingestion against the collection-level fields alone.

repository.name: David M. Rubenstein Rare Book & Manuscript Library, Duke University
repository.unit: Sallie Bingham Center for Women's History & Culture

collection.title: Feminist Women's Health Center Records
collection.summary: Records of a feminist women's health center providing gynecological, contraceptive, and abortion care; includes clinical, educational, and administrative material. Selected by this project as the seed collection because it is the one collection in Duke's reproductive-health holdings whose own description explicitly references birth control and contraception access, rather than abortion-rights advocacy alone.

related_collections_considered.title: Reproductive Rights National Network records
related_collections_considered.note: advocacy-organization newsletters, not a health center's own operational records; less likely fit for a birth-control-history research focus

related_collections_considered.title: William C. Holgate papers
related_collections_considered.note: 19th-century family papers referencing birth control; wrong era for a likely research focus, listed for completeness
